decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 Stability Over New Features
decisions.context: Multiple critical E2 bugs affecting user workflows: Y-axis zoom (ENG-9159), X-axis reset (ENG-9166), dynamic window preview (ENG-9182), calculated channels (ENG-9181, ENG-9184, ENG-9117). Risks customer trust in 'Nail the Core' pillar.

arguments.for: Core product instability directly undermines North Star (Decisions Captured). 7 open UI/UX bugs signal quality debt. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' strategy.
arguments.against: Delays Canvas and Python Rules roadmap items. May slow 'Embed Deeper' initiatives with data pipeline work (ENG-9104).

decisions.title: Accelerate Data Pipeline Infrastructure for K2 Mission
decisions.context: ENG-9104 (K2 data pipeline), ENG-8234 (fresh data reads), ENG-9040 (streaming query results), ENG-9162 (demux cleanup) indicate foundation work for high-velocity data ingestion. Critical for 'Build the Moat' via Sift Edge.
decisions.blockedBy: S3 transfer bucket setup (ENG-9188) and catalog WAL bug (ENG-9114) must resolve first

arguments.for: Enables real-time decision workflows (North Star metric). K2 mission timing externally driven. Differentiates from legacy tools.
arguments.against: Diverts resources from Explore V2 polish. Infrastructure work has long feedback loops.

decisions.title: Invest in Observability Before Tabletop Exercise
decisions.context: ENG-9179 (readiness checklist), ENG-9160 (alerting validation), ENG-9002 (job observability), ENG-8852 (Keycloak alerts), ENG-9169 (grpc error routing) show monitoring gaps ahead of incident response drill.

arguments.for: System reliability enables customer decisions (North Star). Tabletop reveals weaknesses better with monitoring in place. Supports 'Nail the Core'.
arguments.against: Delays feature delivery. Observability work is never 'done'.

decisions.title: Standardize Python Client ABAC for Enterprise Embed
decisions.context: ENG-7055 (Python client ABAC APIs) foundational for 'Embed Deeper' pillar. Enables programmatic access control for Canvas and Python Rules use cases.

arguments.for: Unlocks enterprise deployment patterns. Python Rules depend on secure multi-tenant execution. Moat-building capability.
arguments.against: Low immediate customer visibility. ABAC complexity may slow adoption.

timeAudit.weekSummary: 55% meeting load—above healthy threshold. 3 decision-forcing meetings scheduled but only 8 free hours for synthesis. Consider batch bug triage into single Thursday session.
alerts: Thursday has 7 consecutive meeting hours (9am-4pm) with no break—violates focus time policy
Friday has zero focus blocks—recommend protecting 2-hour morning slot for strategy work
3 one-on-ones scheduled same day as all-hands—may cause calendar fatigue
